perturbation.original_text: Initials: I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with all requirements of the PCII program set out in the CriticalInfrastructure Information Act of 2002 (CII Act) (Title II, Subtitle B, of the Homeland Security Act of 2002, Public Law 107-296, 196 Stat. 2135, 6 USC 101 et seq.), as amended, the implementing regulations thereto (6 CFR Part 29), asamended, and the applicable PCII Procedures Manual, as amended, and with any such requirements that may beofficially communicated to me by the PCII Program Manager or the PCII Program Manager’s designee.
perturbation.changed_text: Initials: I attest that I am familiar with the general concepts of information security.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific reference to the PCII program, the CII Act, its implementing regulations, and the PCII Procedures Manual, the agreement now lacks a clear definition of the standards the signer must comply with, while retaining other references to the PCII program in later sections. This creates an in-text contradiction: The signer agrees to protect PCII (Protected Critical Infrastructure Information) later in the document, but has not agreed to the standards and procedures of the PCII program.
perturbation.location: Page 1, Paragraph starting with Initials: I attest that I am familiar with
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, whichever occurs first, I will surrender promptlyto the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoeverthat is in my possession.(2) If the Authorized Entity is a United States Government contractor performing services in support of the PCII Program, Iwill not request, obtain, maintain, or use PCII unless the PCII Program Manager or Program Manager’s designeehas first made in writing, with respect to the contractor, the certification as provided for in Section 29.8(c) of theimplementing regulations to the CII Act, as amended.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly acknowledges compliance with the PCII program, the CII Act, and related regulations. The changed text replaces this with a general statement about familiarity with information security. This creates a strong contradiction because the contradicted text references specific obligations under the PCII program, which the signer no longer explicitly agrees to in the modified section. As the explanation notes, the signer agrees to protect PCII later in the document, but has not agreed to the standards and procedures of the PCII program, leading to an unenforcable agreement. This falls under the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Other Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU)Initials: As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified information is an over-arching term that covers any information,not otherwise indicated above, which the loss of, misuse of, or unauthorized access to or modification of could adverselyaffect the national interest or the conduct of Federal programs, or the privacy to which individuals are entitled underSection 552a of Title 5, as amended, but which has not been specifically authorized under criteria established by anExecutive Order or an Act of Congress to be kept secret in the interest of national defense or foreign policy. Thisincludes information categorized by DHS or other government agencies as: For Official Use Only (FOUO); Official UseOnly (OUO); Sensitive Homeland Security Information (SHSI); Limited Official Use (LOU); Law EnforcementSensitive (LES); Safeguarding Information (SGI); Unclassified Controlled Nuclear Information (UCNI); and anyother identifier used by other government agencies to categorize information as sensitive but unclassified. I attest that I am familiar with, and I will comply with the standards for access, dissemination, handling, and safeguarding of the information to which I am granted access as cited in this Agreement and in accordance with theguidance provided to me relative to the specific category of information.
perturbation.changed_text: Other Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU)Initials: As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified information is information that requires protection. I attest that I will handle SBU information responsibly.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defined SBU with detail, including examples of categories. By simplifying the definition to 'information that requires protection' and omitting the specific types of SBU and associated handling standards, a contradiction is created. The signer agrees to handle SBU responsibly, but the agreement no longer clearly defines what constitutes SBU or the specific safeguards required creating an in-text contradiction, making enforcement ambiguous and creating an inconsistency with paragraph 5(b).
perturbation.location: Page 1, Paragraph starting with Other Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU)
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered bythis Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorizeddisclosure of or inadvertent access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to thespecific categories of information. I agree that I shall return all information to which I have had access or which is in mypossession 1) upon demand by an authorized individual; and/or 2) upon the conclusion of my duties, association, or supportto DHS; and/or 3) upon the determination that my official duties do not require further access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a detailed definition of SBU, including specific categories and handling standards. The changed text significantly simplifies this definition, stating only that SBU is 'information that requires protection' and removing the specific categories and associated handling standards. This creates a contradiction with section 5(b), which requires the user to handle and safeguard SBU in a manner consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information. Since the changed text no longer clearly defines these specific categories, it's unclear what standards the user must adhere to, creating an in-text contradiction and ambiguity, rendering 5(b) unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 9. (a) With respect to SSI and SBU, I hereby assign to the United States Government all royalties, remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation of the informationnot consistent with the terms of this Agreement.(b) With respect to PCII I hereby assign to the entity owning the PCII and the United States Government, allroyalties, remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure,publication, or revelation of PCII not consistent with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. I hereby assign to the United States Government all royalties, remunerations, and emoluments that have resulted, will result, or may result from any disclosure, publication, or revelation of the information not consistent with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the delineation between SSI/SBU and PCII, the revised text omits the assignment of rights to the 'entity owning the PCII'. The initial text assigned royalties to both the US Government and the entity owning the PCII, which now implies that the Authorized Entity no longer has the right to seek royalties from unauthorized publications, creating an in-text contradiction with section 10.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the Authorized Entity. By granting me conditional access to information in this context, theUnited States Government and, with respect to PCII, the Authorized Entity, may seek any remedy available to it to enforcethis Agreement including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of information in breach ofthis Agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, I could be subjected toadministrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived anystatutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceedingto protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly assigned royalties from PCII disclosures to both the US government and the 'entity owning the PCII'. The changed text removes this assignment to the 'entity owning the PCII', assigning all royalties only to the US Government. Section 10 states that the 'Authorized Entity' may seek remedies to enforce the agreement. By removing the entity's right to royalties from unauthorized publications of PCII, the change creates a contradiction with Section 10, which grants the Authorized Entity the right to enforce the agreement, including seeking remedies, with respect to PCII.

perturbation.original_text: 4. I will not disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or authorization. Should situations arise that warrant the disclosure or release of such information I will do soonly under approved circumstances and in accordance with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable tothe specific categories of information. I will honor and comply with any and all dissemination restrictions cited or verballyrelayed to me by the proper authority.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. I will not disclose or release any information provided to me pursuant to this Agreement without proper authority or authorization.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the second and third sentences, the guidance on *how* disclosure is permitted under certain circumstances is removed, specifically 'in accordance with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information' and 'I will honor and comply with any and all dissemination restrictions cited or verballyrelayed to me by the proper authority'. The agreement retains the prohibition on unauthorized disclosure, but removes the allowance for authorized disclosure and the methods for that disclosure. This now creates an in-text contradiction in relation to clause 14, which makes exception for whistleblowing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. These restrictions are consistent with and do not supersede, conflict with, or otherwise alter the employee obligations, rights, or liabilities created by Executive Order No. 12958, as amended; Section 7211 of Title 5, UnitedStates Code (governing disclosures to Congress); Section 1034 of Title 10, United States Code, as amended by theMilitary Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosure to Congress by members of the military); Section2302(b)(8) of Title 5, United States Code, as amended by the Whistleblower Protection Act (governing disclosures ofillegality, waste, fraud, abuse or public health or safety threats); the Intelligence Identities Protection Act of 1982 (50USC 421 et seq.) (governing disclosures that could expose confidential Government agents); and the statutes whichprotect against disclosure that may compromise the national security, including Sections 641, 793, 794, 798, and 952of Title 18, United States Code, and Section 4(b) of the Subversive Activities Act of 1950 (50 USC 783(b)). Thedefinitions, requirements, obligations, rights, sanctions, and liabilities created by said Executive Order and listedstatutes are incorporated into this agreement and are controlling.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for authorized disclosure of information under specific circumstances (laws, regulations, directives, and dissemination restrictions). The changed text removes this allowance, creating a blanket prohibition on disclosure, regardless of authorization or circumstance. Clause 14, however, explicitly acknowledges and incorporates exceptions for whistleblowing and other legally protected disclosures. The changed text in clause 4 directly contradicts clause 14 by removing the possibility of legally sanctioned disclosures, making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information), I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me bythis Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. I understand that all conditions and obligations imposed upon me by this Agreement apply during the time that I am granted conditional access, and at all times thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'Unless and until I am released in writing by an authorized representative of the Department of Homeland Security (if permissible for the particular category of information),', the agreement implies that there is no way of escaping the obligation, as mentioned in the rest of the clause. This now creates a contradiction in relation to section 5. (b), because upon duty associations, or support to DHS. the agreement can no longer be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered bythis Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorizeddisclosure of or inadvertent access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to thespecific categories of information. I agree that I shall return all information to which I have had access or which is in mypossession 1) upon demand by an authorized individual; and/or 2) upon the conclusion of my duties, association, or supportto DHS; and/or 3) upon the determination that my official duties do not require further access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 11 allows for release from the agreement's obligations by the Department of Homeland Security. The changed text removes this possibility, implying that the obligations are inescapable. This directly contradicts section 5(b), which states that the agreement and its obligations end upon conclusion of duties, association, or support to DHS. Therefore, the omission in section 11 creates a clear contradiction, as it removes a condition that aligns with the termination conditions specified in section 5(b).
